name: Pain au Chocolat (Chocolate Croissants)
instructions: 1. Pour the warm water into a bread machine pan; add the bread flour. Sprinkle the instant nonfat dry milk over flour. 2. Place the sugar, salt, and softened butter into corners of the pan. Make a small indent in dry ingredients and place the instant yeast in the indent. Run the bread machine on the basic dough setting. 3. Meanwhile, shape butter into a 3x5-inch rectangle on waxed paper; wrap and chill. 4. Roll dough onto a lightly floured surface into an 8x12-inch rectangle. Place cold butter on half, fold dough, and press edges to seal. 5. Roll the dough to a 6x14-inch rectangle, fold in thirds, cover, and chill 20 minutes. Repeat and chill for 30 minutes. 6. Beat together half egg yolk and milk. 7. Roll dough into a 12x21-inch rectangle. Cut into thirds in both directions to make 6 rectangles. Divide semisweet chocolate chunks among rectangles. 8. Brush egg yolk mixture on edges, roll around chocolate, press to seal. 9. Place on greased sheets, cover with greased wrap, let rise in warm place until doubled, about 30 minutes. 10. Preheat oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit (200 degrees Celsius). Brush tops with remaining egg yolk mixture. 11. Bake until golden, about 15 minutes. Cool on sheets for 5 minutes, transfer to racks. Serve warm or room temperature.
simpleInstructions: 1. Prepare dough in bread machine with the water, flour, milk, sugar, salt, butter, and yeast. 2. Form and chill butter, then incorporate into rolled dough. Fold, chill, and roll again. 3. Make egg wash. Cut dough into portions, add the chocolate, roll, and seal. 4. Let rise, brush with egg wash, bake at 400 degrees Fahrenheit for 15 mins. Cool and serve.
